# Error Handling Checklist: Runtime Implementation Options and Contract Decision

**Purpose**: Validate error-handling requirement quality for JSON input validation, malformed-envelope diagnostics, exit-code mapping, stdout/stderr separation, missing-runtime behavior, subprocess failures, and XPLAT-004 fixture parity.
**Created**: 2026-06-27
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Is the runner input required to use one versioned JSON stdin envelope with `schema_version`, `request_id`, `helper_id`, `operation`, `mode`, and `inputs`? [Completeness, Spec FR-010, Contract Invocation]
- [x] CHK002 Does the command contract define malformed JSON and missing-required-field handling with exact stdout response, stderr diagnostic, status, exit code, and diagnostic-code expectations? [Resolved, Completeness, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-011, Spec FR-012, Spec FR-018a, Contract Fixture Parity Expectations]
- [x] CHK003 Is stdout required to emit exactly one versioned JSON response rather than human-readable diagnostics? [Completeness, Spec Clarifications Session 2, Contract Stdout Response]
- [x] CHK004 Is stderr required to use deterministic line-delimited JSON diagnostic events? [Completeness, Spec FR-011, Contract Stderr Diagnostics]
- [x] CHK005 Are `legacy_exit_code` requirements defined for helper-specific parity without replacing the shared process exit-code map? [Completeness, Spec FR-012, Contract Exit-Code Map]

## Exit-Code Mapping

- [x] CHK006 Is success mapped to exit code `0`? [Clarity, Spec FR-012, Contract Exit-Code Map]
- [x] CHK007 Are expected helper or domain failures mapped to exit code `1`? [Clarity, Spec FR-012, Contract Exit-Code Map]
- [x] CHK008 Are input envelope, usage, malformed JSON, and schema errors mapped to exit code `2`? [Clarity, Spec FR-012, Contract Exit-Code Map]
- [x] CHK009 Are missing selected runtime, missing executable, or missing input prerequisite cases mapped to exit code `3`? [Clarity, Spec FR-012, Contract Exit-Code Map]
- [x] CHK010 Are subprocess nonzero, timeout, and stderr-only failure categories mapped to exit code `4` unless explicitly classified as expected helper/domain failure? [Clarity, Spec FR-012, Contract Subprocess Rules]
- [x] CHK011 Are unexpected runner exceptions or unclassified internal failures mapped to exit code `5`? [Clarity, Spec FR-012, Contract Exit-Code Map]

## Stdout and Stderr Separation

- [x] CHK012 Are diagnostics kept out of stdout so successful output remains parseable JSON? [Consistency, Spec FR-011, Contract Stdout Response]
- [x] CHK013 Are stderr diagnostic events required to include `severity`, `code`, `message`, `source`, and `details`? [Clarity, Spec Clarifications Session 2, Contract Stderr Diagnostics]
- [x] CHK014 Is line-delimited stderr compatible with one diagnostic object per line rather than multiline prose? [Clarity, Contract Stderr Diagnostics]
- [x] CHK015 Does the contract preserve parseable stdout for failed invocations as well as successful invocations where the runner can safely emit a response? [Coverage, Spec Clarifications Session 2, Contract Stdout Response]

## Subprocess and Missing-Command Behavior

- [x] CHK016 Are subprocesses required to use structured argv arrays with shell disabled? [Completeness, Spec FR-014, Contract Subprocess Rules]
- [x] CHK017 Are shell interpolation, globbing, redirection, `.sh`, and `jq` fallbacks excluded from the selected contract? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications Session 2, Contract Subprocess Rules]
- [x] CHK018 Are subprocess result fields sufficient for diagnostics and parity fixtures: argv, cwd, env, stdout, stderr, exit code, timed-out state, and missing-prerequisite state? [Completeness, Data Model Subprocess Result, Contract Subprocess Rules]
- [x] CHK019 Are missing executables mapped to missing prerequisites rather than generic subprocess failures? [Clarity, Contract Subprocess Rules]
- [x] CHK020 Are subprocess nonzero and timeout failures mapped separately from missing executable failures? [Coverage, Contract Subprocess Rules]

## Fixture Parity Readiness

- [x] CHK021 Are success, invalid JSON, missing required field, path-with-spaces, Windows separator, missing-prerequisite, subprocess-nonzero, stderr-only failure, runtime-info/preflight, and one read-only legacy-helper comparison all named as XPLAT-004 fixture parity expectations? [Completeness, Spec FR-018a, Contract Fixture Parity Expectations]
- [x] CHK022 Is the fixture list bounded to runner contract behavior rather than implementing helpers in XPLAT-002? [Consistency, Spec FR-020, Contract Fixture Parity Expectations]
- [x] CHK023 Are missing runtime and missing command behaviors covered through prerequisite and subprocess categories without requiring public support-claim changes? [Coverage, Spec FR-012, Spec FR-019, Contract Exit-Code Map]
- [x] CHK024 Does quickstart validation ask reviewers to check stderr/exit separation, shell-free subprocess or missing-command behavior, and fixture parity expectations without running or implementing the runner? [Acceptance Criteria, Quickstart]

## Notes

- Initial checklist pass found one true gap: malformed-envelope and fixture-parity assertions were named but not precise enough for XPLAT-004 to build parity tests from requirement text alone.
- Remediation added input-validation response rules, allowed response statuses,
  stable diagnostic codes, missing-runtime classification, and fixture-level
  stdout/stderr/exit assertions to the contract, spec, data model, plan,
  research, and quickstart.

## Rerun Verification - Loop 1

- [x] CHK025 Do malformed JSON and missing-required-field fixtures now have exact stdout `status`, process `exit_code`, stderr diagnostic `code`, and response-field expectations? [Resolved, Contract Input Validation and Error Responses, Contract Fixture Parity Expectations]
- [x] CHK026 Do subprocess nonzero, timeout, stderr-only failure, missing prerequisite, and missing runtime cases now have stable status/exit/diagnostic assertions for XPLAT-004 fixture authors? [Resolved, Contract Stderr Diagnostics, Contract Exit-Code Map, Contract Fixture Parity Expectations]
- [x] CHK027 Does the remediation remain limited to decision and contract artifacts without implementing runner behavior or changing public support-claim surfaces? [Consistency, Spec FR-019, Spec FR-020, Quickstart]
